svg.title: Proposal overview figure for cardiovascular hemodynamics reconstruction
svg.desc: Wide overview figure showing multimodal cardiovascular imaging flowing into an information field theory engine, a validation pathway, and four specific aims.

text.tspan: of cardiovascular flow from multimodal imaging

svg.text: Infer patient-specific geometry, velocity, pressure, and uncertainty from noisy PC-MRI, 4D Flow MRI, and Doppler Echo.
Direct measurements are informative, but noisy, partial,
spatially averaged, and often hard to co-register.
captures complex flow over the cardiac cycle
Bayesian inference over fields fuses data with physics without relying on a CFD solver in the loop.

text.tspan: Eikonal, continuity, Navier-Stokes, and boundary-condition Hamiltonians
Forward models for PC-MRI, 4D Flow MRI, Echo, and modality alignment
Adaptive spatial bases plus Markovian time evolution with initial conditions
Preconditioned SGLD and amortized VI with Monte Carlo gradients and batching

svg.text: geometry, velocity, pressure, modality transforms, and uncertainty
Improve medical image analysis, risk assessment, and clinical decisions.
Organized from formulation and representation to scalable inference and real-world demonstration.
priors for geometry, velocity, and pressure
forward models for PC-MRI, 4D Flow, and Echo
spatial fields with explicit boundary conditions
Markovian time evolution plus initial conditions
in vitro phantoms benchmarked by PIV/PTV
